evals.prompt: I need to parse GS1 AI barcode data in my shared KMP module. Here's my model: EmptyParserModel.kt. Create the Parser and add a parse(input: String) function that returns the parsed fields.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads references/integration.md and writes a Parser created via the forFormat factory for GS1_AI, with a parse function that calls parseString and returns/exposes the ParsedData.

assertions.text: import com.kmp.datacapture.parser.Parser is present
import com.kmp.datacapture.parser.ParserDataFormat is present
Parser.forFormat( is called to create the parser
ParserDataFormat.GS1_AI is passed as the format
the existing dataCaptureContext field is passed into Parser.forFormat, not a newly constructed DataCaptureContext
com.scandit.datacapture.parser.Parser (the native, non-KMP package) is NOT used
parser.parseString( is called inside the new parse function
a ParsedData value is returned or exposed from the parse function

evals.prompt: After parsing, I want to loop over every field and print its name and parsed value, plus look up one field directly by name. Use EmptyParserModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill shows iterating parsedData.fields for name/parsed, and a fieldsByName[...] lookup for the by-name field access, matching the ParsedData/ParsedField shapes in references/integration.md.

assertions.text: parsedData.fields is iterated (e.g. a for loop or forEach over .fields)
field.name is read inside the loop
field.parsed is read inside the loop
parsedData.fieldsByName[ (or .fieldsByName.get() equivalent) is used for the by-name lookup
field.rawString or field.warnings is not fabricated with properties that don't exist on ParsedField (only name, parsed, rawString, warnings are used)

evals.prompt: My parseString call can throw. I want to catch the Scandit-specific error and show its code and message to the user, and also report any fields that came back with warnings even when parsing succeeds. Use EmptyParserModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill wraps parseString in a try/catch that catches ParserException specifically (reading code, message, additionalInfo) before falling back to a generic Exception catch, and separately reads parsedData.fieldsWithIssues / field.warnings for successful-but-warned fields.

assertions.text: import com.kmp.datacapture.parser.ParserException is present
catch (e: ParserException) is present
e.code is read inside the ParserException catch block
e.message is read inside the ParserException catch block
the ParserException catch block appears before a broader catch (e: Exception) block, not after
parsedData.fieldsWithIssues is read to find fields with warnings
field.warnings is iterated for the fields with issues

evals.prompt: I'm already scanning barcodes with BarcodeCapture in ScanAndParseModel.kt. Every time a barcode is scanned, run its data through a HIBC Parser and store the parsed result. Don't touch the existing BarcodeCapture wiring.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a HIBC Parser and calls parser.parseString(barcode.data) inside onBarcodeScanned, storing/exposing the ParsedData, while leaving the existing BarcodeCaptureListener overrides, session handling, and lastBarcodeData flow untouched.

assertions.text: ParserDataFormat.HIBC is passed when creating the parser
Parser.forFormat( is called to create the parser
barcode.data is passed into parser.parseString( inside onBarcodeScanned
the existing onBarcodeScanned override signature (barcodeCapture, session, data) is preserved
the existing onSessionUpdated override is preserved
session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is still read to obtain the barcode
the existing _lastBarcodeData / lastBarcodeData StateFlow is preserved
camera setup or BarcodeCaptureSettings configuration is NOT added or modified (out of scope, already wired)

evals.prompt: I have a driver's license PDF417 barcode and I want to use the KMP Parser to pull out the AAMVA fields like name and date of birth. Use EmptyParserModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that ParserDataFormat has no AAMVA value and this generic Parser does not decode AAMVA driver's license data, and routes the user to an ID Capture skill instead of inventing a ParserDataFormat.AAMVA value or fabricating AAMVA field names.

assertions.text: the response states that ParserDataFormat has no AAMVA value / AAMVA is not a supported Parser data format
ParserDataFormat.AAMVA is NOT used as a code literal anywhere in the response
the response directs the user to ID Capture (or an id-capture-* skill) for driver's license/AAMVA data instead of the Parser

evals.prompt: I want my Swiss QR parser to run in strict mode and only accept minimal version 2.0 bills. Use EmptyParserModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a Parser for SWISS_QR and calls setOptions with a plain Map containing strictMode and minimalVersion keys, matching the option keys documented in references/integration.md.

assertions.text: ParserDataFormat.SWISS_QR is passed when creating the parser
parser.setOptions( is called
the options map passed to setOptions includes a strictMode key
the options map passed to setOptions includes a minimalVersion key
setOptions is called with a Map literal (mapOf(...) or equivalent), not a builder class
